eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent while Myntra and Meesho show strong but discount-distorted demand.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong presence
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present in full-price segment.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong rank and reviews but at 65% discount; demand is price-driven.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong volume at ₹299; demand exists in value segment.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is there genuine style demand, or is all demand driven by deep discounts and low price?
conflicts.proves: The style has traction in discount-heavy and value channels.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove willingness to pay full price or premium positioning.

conflicts.title: Myntra demand is Subsidized Liquidation vs Meesho genuine value demand
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank but 65% off, 3.2 rating; discount IS demand.
conflicts.meesho_says: 8000+ units at ₹299, 3.6 rating; volume suggests genuine value demand.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Myntra demand purely discount-driven, or does it reflect underlying style preference?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists at low price points, but quality differs between channels.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that Myntra buyers would purchase without deep discount.

convergences.title: Both Myntra and Meesho show strong presence

convergences.proves: The style has broad market visibility and sales volume.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand is style-driven rather than price-driven.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume at ₹299 with 120 resellers, indicating grassroots demand in value fashion. Myntra's high rank suggests the style has visual appeal that drives clicks even if purchases are discount-motivated.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Nykaa absence signals no premium demand. Myntra demand is heavily distorted by 65% discount (Subsidized Liquidation). The style may be a price-only play with no brand equity or full-price potential.

nykaa.reason: No data; absence is a signal but cannot confirm zero demand.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: High volume, decent rating, reseller base; clean value demand signal.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: Meesho volume suggests demand at low price, but Nykaa absence and Myntra distortion indicate no premium or style-driven demand. A small trial in value channels can validate if the style works at a modest price without deep discount.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would the style sell at a moderate price (₹500-700) without heavy discounting?

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge on strong demand for golden kurtis across price tiers with clean signals

convergences.title: Strong multi-channel demand
convergences.detail: Nykaa, Myntra, and Meesho all show strong presence with positive ratings and low discount distortion, indicating genuine demand across premium, mass, and value segments.
convergences.proves: Broad consumer interest in golden kurtis is real and not driven by heavy discounting.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the demand is for the same style or different interpretations of 'golden kurti' across channels.

convergences.title: Clean discount signals
convergences.detail: Nykaa (5% off), Myntra (10% off, Strategic Value Pricing), and Meesho (no discount mentioned) all show minimal discount distortion, suggesting demand is style-driven.
convergences.proves: Purchases are likely based on product appeal rather than price promotions.
convergences.cannot_prove: Long-term price elasticity or willingness to pay higher prices.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden kurtis show strong, clean demand across premium (Nykaa), mass (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels with minimal discount distortion. Myntra's high rank and low discount indicate style-driven purchase intent. Meesho's high volume and accelerating reseller growth suggest expanding reach in tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data to validate against own store performance. The price gap between Nykaa (₹1450) and Meesho (₹349) may indicate different product interpretations or consumer segments. Reseller growth on Meesho could inflate volume figures.

fusion-palazzo.headline: All sources converge on strong demand, but Nykaa absence and paid visibility on Myntra introduce minor conflicts.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent while other sources strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No products found; trend absent in premium D2C.
conflicts.internal_says: Proven success at similar price point.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this trend only viable for mass-market and value-fashion, or can it be pushed to premium channels?
conflicts.proves: Trend is mass-market, not premium; Nykaa's absence confirms no premium demand.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether premium demand could be created with different styling or branding.

conflicts.title: Myntra/Ajio paid visibility and discount may inflate demand signal
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank with 35-40% discount and sponsored tags.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong volume at lower price without discount.
conflicts.internal_says: Strong sell-through without heavy discount (12% markdown).
conflicts.buyer_question: How much of Myntra's demand is driven by discount and ads vs genuine style preference?
conflicts.proves: Discount is accelerating a genuine trend (Genuine Volume Driver), but paid visibility reduces rank reliability.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether organic demand at full price would be as strong.

convergences.title: Strong demand across mass-market channels
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio and Meesho both show strong presence and volume; internal POS confirms success.
convergences.proves: Trend has broad appeal across price points and regions.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable long-term or if design fatigue will set in.

convergences.title: Set format is key value proposition
convergences.proves: Internal POS shows high repeat rate and low returns; Meesho volume confirms value perception.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether individual kurtis would perform as well without palazzo.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: Strong convergence across Myntra, Meesho, and internal POS indicates robust demand for fusion kurti with palazzo sets. The set format drives value perception, leading to high sell-through and repeat purchases. Internal data shows stockouts in 68 stores, suggesting unmet demand that can be captured.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence signals no premium demand, limiting price ceiling. Myntra's paid visibility and discount may inflate apparent demand; organic full-price demand is unproven. Meesho's lower rating (3.6) hints at quality concerns that could affect returns.

nykaa.reason: No data available; cannot assess demand at premium tier.

myntra.reason: Strong signal but paid visibility and discount introduce noise; rank weight halved.

meesho.reason: High volume, many resellers, broad regional coverage; rating is moderate but acceptable for value segment.

internal.reason: High sell-through, good margin, low returns, strong repeat rate; ground truth data.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: Multiple clean signals converge: Myntra and Meesho show strong demand, internal POS confirms success with stockouts. The set format is a proven value prop. Expand to more stores and regions.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will next season's designs maintain the same velocity, or will design fatigue reduce demand?

fusion-palazzo.demand_integrity: Genuine Volume Driver with Paid Visibility; discount accelerates real trend, but rank weight reduced due to sponsorship.
